1→# Code Insights Vision
     2→
     3→## Philosophy
     4→
     5→**Your data, your machine, your insights.**
     6→
     7→Code Insights is a free, open-source tool that helps developers who use multiple AI coding tools analyze their sessions, collect insights, track decisions and learnings, and build knowledge over time. It's built on a simple principle: your session data never leaves your machine.
     8→
     9→## Core Beliefs
    10→
    11→### 1. Privacy by Architecture
    12→
    13→There is no central Code Insights server. No accounts, no sign-ups, no cloud. All session data lives in a local SQLite database at `~/.code-insights/data.db`. The dashboard runs locally at `http://localhost:7890` — it never phones home.
    14→
    15→### 2. Developers Can Handle It
    16→
    17→Developers using AI coding tools are technical. They can:
    18→- Run `code-insights init` and answer three questions
    19→- Install a post-session hook with one command
    20→- Open a local dashboard that just works
    21→
    22→We don't need to hide complexity behind a managed service. Clear documentation beats magic.
    23→
    24→### 3. Single-Repo, Local-First
    25→
    26→Everything ships in one repository:
    27→- **CLI** (open source, MIT) — the parser, sync engine, and stats commands
    28→- **Dashboard** (embedded SPA) — served locally by a Hono server via `code-insights dashboard`
    29→- **Server** (local API) — Hono API on `localhost:7890`, proxies LLM calls server-side
    30→
    31→No hosted infrastructure. No Vercel. No Firebase. No Supabase. One install, zero cloud dependencies.
    32→
    33→### 4. Tool, Not Platform
    34→
    35→Code Insights is a utility, not a product. It should:
    36→- Do one thing well (extract insights from AI coding sessions)
    37→- Support multiple source tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI)
    38→- Be easy to install and configure
    39→- Stay out of the way once set up
    40→
    41→## Long-Term Direction
    42→
    43→### Phase 1: Foundation ✅
    44→- CLI tool that parses JSONL → SQLite
    45→- Web dashboard with session views, character classification, smart titles
    46→- Claude Code hook for automatic session sync
    47→
    48→### Phase 2: Integration ✅
    49→- Auto-sync via Claude Code post-session hook
    50→- CLI stats command suite (`stats`, `stats cost`, `stats projects`, `stats today`, `stats models`)
    51→- Terminal analytics powered by local SQLite
    52→
    53→### Phase 3: Intelligence ✅
    54→- Multi-provider LLM analysis (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama)
    55→- On-demand and bulk session analysis
    56→- Cross-session insight types (summary, decision, learning, technique)
    57→
    58→### Phase 4: Feature Parity ✅
    59→- Vite + React SPA replacing the hosted web dashboard
    60→- Hono server embedding the SPA — served via `code-insights dashboard`
    61→- Multi-source support: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI
    62→- Full feature parity between CLI stats and dashboard views
    63→
    64→### Phase 5: Telemetry (In Progress)
    65→- Anonymous aggregate usage signals (opt-in, local-only export)
    66→- Depth metrics: sessions analyzed, insights generated, knowledge retained
    67→
    68→### Phase 6: Polish & Distribution
    69→- npm publish as `@code-insights/cli`
    70→- Static landing page at `code-insights.app`
    71→- Contribution guidelines and plugin architecture foundation
    72→
    73→## Non-Goals
    74→
    75→- **Not a business** — No monetization, no paywall, no premium tier
    76→- **Not a central platform** — No central database for user session data
    77→- **Not a dependency** — Users can stop using it anytime, data remains theirs
    78→- **Not a team tool** — This is a personal learning tool; no org/team features
    79→
    80→## Success Looks Like
    81→
    82→A developer installs Code Insights, runs `code-insights init`, installs the hook, and from then on has a local dashboard showing:
    83→- What they built with AI coding tools this week
    84→- Key decisions and why they made them
    85→- Patterns in how they use AI assistance across tools
    86→
    87→They own all the data. They can export it. They can delete it. They can modify the CLI tool. Complete autonomy.
    88→

1→# Code Insights
     2→
     3→## What It Is
     4→
     5→Code Insights transforms your AI coding session history into structured, searchable insights. It extracts patterns from your conversations—what you built, decisions you made, lessons learned—and presents them in a local visual dashboard.
     6→
     7→## The Problem
     8→
     9→AI coding tools store every conversation as files on your machine. Claude Code uses JSONL in `~/.claude/projects/`. Cursor stores state in SQLite. Codex CLI and Copilot CLI write their own session formats. This is valuable data:
    10→- What features did you work on last week?
    11→- Why did you choose that architecture?
    12→- What mistakes did you make (and fix)?
    13→- How much time went into different parts of the codebase?
    14→
    15→But it's trapped in raw files. You can't search it, visualize it, or learn from it.
    16→
    17→## The Solution
    18→
    19→Code Insights provides:
    20→
    21→1. **Automated extraction** — Parses session files from multiple AI coding tools and structures the data
    22→2. **Smart session titles** — Auto-generates meaningful titles from session content
    23→3. **Session classification** — Categorizes sessions (deep focus, bug hunt, feature build, etc.)
    24→4. **LLM-powered analysis** — Multi-provider insight generation (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama) with your own API key
    25→5. **Visual dashboard** — Local web interface with charts, timelines, and filters at `http://localhost:7890`
    26→6. **CLI analytics** — Terminal stats via `code-insights stats` and subcommands
    27→
    28→## Who It's For
    29→
    30→- **Developers using multiple AI coding tools** who want to understand their AI-assisted work patterns across Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, and Copilot CLI
    31→- **Learners** who want to review and reinforce what they've built with AI assistants
    32→- **Privacy-conscious developers** who want insights without giving up their data to a cloud service
    33→
    34→## Privacy Model
    35→
    36→**Fully local. No cloud. No accounts.**
    37→
    38→Code Insights stores all session data in a SQLite database at `~/.code-insights/data.db` on your own machine. There is no central server, no sign-up, and no data sent anywhere. The dashboard runs locally at `http://localhost:7890` — served by a Hono API process on your own machine.
    39→
    40→LLM analysis uses your own API key, stored in `~/.code-insights/config.json` (mode 0o600). API calls go directly from the local server to your chosen LLM provider — not through any Code Insights infrastructure.
    41→
    42→## Core Features
    43→
    44→### Multi-Source Support
    45→
    46→| Source Tool | What's Captured |
    47→|-------------|-----------------|
    48→| **Claude Code** | JSONL sessions from `~/.claude/projects/` |
    49→| **Cursor** | Sessions from Cursor's local SQLite state |
    50→| **Codex CLI** | Rollout files from `~/.codex/sessions/` |
    51→| **Copilot CLI** | Event files from `~/.copilot/session-state/` |
    52→
    53→### Insight Categories
    54→
    55→| Category | What It Captures |
    56→|----------|------------------|
    57→| **Summary** | High-level narrative of what was accomplished |
    58→| **Decision** | Architecture choices, trade-offs, reasoning, alternatives |
    59→| **Learning** | Technical discoveries, mistakes, transferable knowledge |
    60→| **Technique** | Problem-solving approaches and debugging strategies |
    61→
    62→### Dashboard Views
    63→
    64→- **Daily/Weekly digest** — Summary of recent sessions
    65→- **Project timeline** — Visual history of work per project
    66→- **Decision log** — Searchable archive of "why" decisions
    67→- **Analytics** — Charts showing effort distribution, patterns
    68→- **Session detail** — Full session with analyze button for LLM insights
    69→
    70→### CLI Stats Commands
    71→
    72→```bash
    73→code-insights stats              # Overview (last 7 days)
    74→code-insights stats cost         # Cost breakdown by project and model
    75→code-insights stats projects     # Per-project detail cards
    76→code-insights stats today        # Today's sessions with details
    77→code-insights stats models       # Model usage distribution
    78→```
    79→
    80→## Tech Stack
    81→
    82→- **CLI**: Node.js (ES2022, ES Modules), Commander.js
    83→- **Database**: SQLite (`better-sqlite3`) at `~/.code-insights/data.db` — WAL mode, local
    84→- **Server**: Hono — lightweight API server, serves dashboard SPA at `localhost:7890`
    85→- **Dashboard**: Vite + React 19 SPA, Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui
    86→- **AI**: Multi-provider — OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama (your own API keys, proxied server-side)
    87→- **Package manager**: pnpm (workspace monorepo: `cli/`, `dashboard/`, `server/`)
    88→
    89→## Success Metrics
    90→
    91→- Time to first insight: < 5 minutes from install
    92→- User can answer "what did I work on this week?" in one click
    93→- Decisions are searchable and linkable
    94→- Zero cloud dependencies after install
    95→

1→# Code Insights Roadmap
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→This roadmap outlines the development phases for Code Insights. Timelines are flexible—progress is driven by priorities and availability.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Phase 1: Foundation ✅
    10→
    11→**Goal:** Working end-to-end flow from AI session files to local dashboard
    12→
    13→### Milestones
    14→
    15→- [x] **1.1 Project Setup**
    16→  - Single-repo pnpm workspace monorepo (`cli/`, `dashboard/`, `server/`)
    17→  - TypeScript strict mode, ES Modules
    18→  - Configuration system at `~/.code-insights/config.json`
    19→
    20→- [x] **1.2 Claude Code Parser**
    21→  - Parse Claude Code session files from `~/.claude/projects/`
    22→  - Extract: sessions, messages, tool calls, timestamps
    23→  - Smart session title generation (5-tier fallback)
    24→  - Session character classification (7 types)
    25→  - CLI command: `code-insights sync`
    26→
    27→- [x] **1.3 SQLite Schema & Sync**
    28→  - Local database at `~/.code-insights/data.db` (WAL mode)
    29→  - Tables: projects, sessions, messages, insights
    30→  - Incremental sync (tracks file modification times in `sync-state.json`)
    31→  - Stable project IDs derived from git remote URLs
    32→  - CLI command: `code-insights init`
    33→
    34→- [x] **1.4 Basic Dashboard**
    35→  - Session list view with filters (project, date)
    36→  - Session detail view with message display
    37→  - Insights display by type
    38→  - Analytics page with Recharts charts
    39→
    40→### Deliverables
    41→- ✅ CLI tool that syncs sessions to local SQLite
    42→- ✅ Local web dashboard served by `code-insights dashboard`
    43→
    44→---
    45→
    46→## Phase 2: Integration ✅
    47→
    48→**Goal:** Seamless integration with Claude Code workflow and terminal analytics
    49→
    50→### Milestones
    51→
    52→- [x] **2.1 Claude Code Hook**
    53→  - Post-session hook that triggers sync automatically
    54→  - Quiet mode for background processing (`sync -q`)
    55→  - `code-insights install-hook` / `code-insights uninstall-hook`
    56→
    57→- [x] **2.2 CLI Stats Command Suite**
    58→  - Terminal analytics: `stats`, `stats cost`, `stats projects`, `stats today`, `stats models`
    59→  - Powered by local SQLite — zero external dependencies
    60→  - Unicode sparklines, bar charts, semantic colors, responsive layout
    61→  - Shared flags: `--period`, `--project`, `--source`, `--no-sync`
    62→
    63→- [ ] **2.3 Enhanced Filtering**
    64→  - Full-text search across sessions
    65→  - Filter by: project, git branch, date range, insight type, source tool
    66→  - Saved filters / bookmarks
    67→
    68→### Deliverables
    69→- ✅ Auto-sync via Claude Code hook
    70→- ✅ CLI stats command suite with local analytics
    71→- Enhanced filtering (pending)
    72→
    73→---
    74→
    75→## Phase 3: Intelligence ✅
    76→
    77→**Goal:** LLM-powered deeper insights
    78→
    79→### Milestones
    80→
    81→- [x] **3.1 Multi-Provider LLM Integration**
    82→  - Pluggable provider system (factory pattern)
    83→  - OpenAI (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4-turbo)
    84→  - Anthropic (claude-sonnet, claude-haiku, claude-opus)
    85→  - Google Gemini (gemini-2.0-flash, gemini-1.5-pro/flash)
    86→  - Ollama for local models (llama3.2, mistral, codellama)
    87→  - LLM calls proxied server-side via Hono API (no browser CORS)
    88→  - Token input capped at 80k
    89→
    90→- [x] **3.2 Session Analysis**
    91→  - "Analyze" button on session detail page
    92→  - Bulk analyze for unanalyzed sessions
    93→  - Generates 4 insight types: summary, decision, learning, technique
    94→  - Analysis versioning for re-analysis
    95→
    96→- [ ] **3.3 Cross-Session Patterns**
    97→  - Cross-session pattern detection
    98→  - Project-level and overall-level insights
    99→  - Recurring pattern identification
   100→
   101→- [ ] **3.4 Learning Journal**
   102→  - Auto-generate "lessons learned" from sessions
   103→  - Track recurring patterns and mistakes
   104→  - Suggest areas for improvement
   105→
   106→### Deliverables
   107→- ✅ Multi-provider LLM insight generation
   108→- ✅ On-demand and bulk session analysis
   109→- Cross-session patterns (pending)
   110→- Learning journal (pending)
   111→
   112→---
   113→
   114→## Phase 4: Feature Parity ✅
   115→
   116→**Goal:** Port all dashboard features to the embedded local SPA and add multi-source support
   117→
   118→### Milestones
   119→
   120→- [x] **4.1 Vite + React SPA Foundation**
   121→  - Vite SPA replacing Next.js (no SSR needed for localhost)
   122→  - Hono API server embedding the SPA
   123→  - React Query for server state, Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui
   124→
   125→- [x] **4.2 Multi-Source Provider Support**
   126→  - `SessionProvider` interface for source tool abstraction
   127→  - Providers: `claude-code`, `cursor`, `codex-cli`, `copilot-cli`
   128→  - `--source` filter flag in all stats and sync commands
   129→  - Source tool display in dashboard (colors, avatars, filters)
   130→
   131→- [x] **4.3 Dashboard Feature Parity**
   132→  - Sessions list with source, project, date, character filters
   133→  - Session detail with full message display and analyze button
   134→  - Analytics page with cost, models, projects breakdown
   135→  - Stats overview and project cards
   136→

1→# Multi-Tool Vision: Beyond Claude Code
     2→
     3→> Expanding Code Insights to capture sessions from all AI coding tools.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Motivation
     8→
     9→Code Insights was built to capture insights from Claude Code sessions. But developers increasingly use multiple AI coding tools — sometimes within the same project. The conversation data from these tools is trapped in proprietary formats, scattered across different locations, and inaccessible for reflection.
    10→
    11→**The opportunity:** Become the universal session insights layer for AI-assisted development, regardless of which tool generated the conversation.
    12→
    13→## Target Tools
    14→
    15→| Tool | Type | Storage Format | Local? | Feasibility |
    16→|------|------|---------------|--------|-------------|
    17→| Claude Code | CLI | JSONL | Yes | **Already supported** |
    18→| Cursor | IDE (VS Code fork) | SQLite + JSON blobs | Yes | **High** (Phase 1) |
    19→| OpenAI Codex CLI | CLI | JSONL | Yes | **High** (Phase 2) |
    20→| GitHub Copilot | VS Code extension | JSON in workspaceStorage | Yes | **Medium-Low** (Phase 3) |
    21→| Gemini CLI | CLI | TBD (investigate) | TBD | **Unknown** (Phase 4) |
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## How Each Tool Stores Conversations
    26→
    27→### Claude Code (Current)
    28→
    29→- **Location**: `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-project-path>/<session-id>.jsonl`
    30→- **Format**: JSONL — one JSON object per line, append-only
    31→- **Entry types**: `user`, `assistant`, `system`, `summary`
    32→- **Key fields**: `uuid`, `type`, `message.content`, `timestamp`, `cwd`, `gitBranch`, `version`
    33→- **Session discovery**: Walk `~/.claude/projects/` directories, each `.jsonl` file is one session
    34→- **Platform paths**:
    35→  - macOS/Linux: `~/.claude/projects/`
    36→  - Windows: `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\projects\`
    37→
    38→### Cursor
    39→
    40→- **Location**: SQLite databases (`state.vscdb`) in two locations:
    41→  - Global: `<cursor-data-dir>/globalStorage/state.vscdb`
    42→  - Per-workspace: `<cursor-data-dir>/workspaceStorage/<hash>/state.vscdb`
    43→- **Platform-specific data directory**:
    44→  - macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/`
    45→  - Linux: `~/.config/Cursor/User/`
    46→  - Windows: `%APPDATA%\Cursor\User\`
    47→- **Format**: SQLite key-value table (`ItemTable`) with JSON blobs as values
    48→- **Key DB keys**:
    49→  - `composer.composerData` — primary chat list (v3 format)
    50→  - `composerData:<composerId>` — individual conversation data
    51→  - `workbench.panel.aichat.view.aichat.chatdata` — legacy chat storage
    52→- **Message schema** (composerData v3):
    53→  ```json
    54→  {
    55→    "bubbleId": "uuid",
    56→    "type": 2,
    57→    "createdAt": "ISO timestamp",
    58→    "text": "message content",
    59→    "codeBlocks": [...],
    60→    "toolFormerData": {...},
    61→    "thinking": {...}
    62→  }
    63→  ```
    64→  - `type: 1` = user message, `type: 2` = assistant response
    65→- **Challenges**:
    66→  - Schema is undocumented, has changed across versions (v1 → v3)
    67→  - Must open SQLite in read-only mode while Cursor is running
    68→  - Workspace hash directories require resolution to actual project paths
    69→- **Community tools**: [cursor-view](https://github.com/saharmor/cursor-view), [cursor-db-mcp](https://github.com/jbdamask/cursor-db-mcp), [cursor-conversations-mcp](https://www.pulsemcp.com/servers/cursor-chat-history)
    70→
    71→### OpenAI Codex CLI
    72→
    73→- **Location**: `~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl`
    74→- **Format**: JSONL — event-based stream, append-only
    75→- **Event types**: `thread.started`, `turn.started`, `turn.completed`, `turn.failed`, `item.*`, `error`
    76→- **Key fields**: Per-turn token counts (input, cached, output, reasoning), model info
    77→- **Session discovery**: Walk `~/.codex/sessions/` by date directories
    78→- **Resume**: `codex resume --all` lists all sessions
    79→- **Challenges**:
    80→  - Event schema not fully documented publicly
    81→  - Rollout format may differ between CLI and VS Code extension versions
    82→- **Community tools**: [ccusage](https://github.com/ryoppippi/ccusage) (token tracking), [ccmanager](https://github.com/kbwo/ccmanager) (session management)
    83→
    84→### GitHub Copilot
    85→
    86→- **Location**: VS Code's internal workspace storage
    87→  - macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/workspaceStorage/<hash>/chatSessions/*.json`
    88→  - Linux: `~/.config/Code/User/workspaceStorage/<hash>/chatSessions/*.json`
    89→  - Windows: `%APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage/<hash>/chatSessions/*.json`
    90→- **Format**: JSON files per workspace
    91→- **Challenges**:
    92→  - Schema is not publicly documented and varies across extension versions
    93→  - Workspace directories use opaque hashes (need to discover/map to project paths)
    94→  - No native export CLI — only VS Code command palette (`Chat: Export Session...`)
    95→  - Agent mode sessions may use different storage than regular chat
    96→  - GitHub has been asked repeatedly for better history/export — still limited
    97→- **Workarounds**: Third-party extensions like SpecStory auto-save to `.specstory/` directories
    98→
    99→---
   100→
   101→## Architecture: Provider Abstraction
   102→
   103→The CLI needs a **provider abstraction** — a common interface that each tool's parser implements:
   104→
   105→```
   106→┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   107→│                    CLI Pipeline                           │
   108→│                                                          │
   109→│  ┌─────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌────────────┐  │
   110→│  │  Discovery   │───▶│   Parser     │───▶│  Sync to   │  │
   111→│  │  (find files)│    │  (normalize) │    │  Firestore │  │
   112→│  └─────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └────────────┘  │
   113→│         │                  │                              │
   114→│         ▼                  ▼                              │
   115→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────┐                     │
   116→│  │      Provider Interface         │                     │
   117→│  │                                 │                     │
   118→│  │  discover() → FilePath[]        │                     │
   119→│  │  parse(file) → ParsedSession    │                     │
   120→│  │  getProviderName() → string     │                     │
   121→│  └─────────────────────────────────┘                     │
   122→│         ▲         ▲         ▲                            │
   123→│  ┌──────┴──┐ ┌────┴────┐ ┌─┴───────┐                    │
   124→│  │ Claude  │ │ Cursor  │ │ Codex   │  ...                │
   125→│  │Provider │ │Provider │ │Provider │                     │
   126→│  └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘                    │
   127→└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   128→```
   129→
   130→Each provider must normalize its native format into the existing `ParsedSession` and `ParsedMessage` types. This means mapping tool-specific concepts to our schema:
   131→
   132→| Our Field | Claude Code | Cursor | Codex CLI | Copilot |
   133→|-----------|-------------|--------|-----------|---------|
   134→| `sessionId` | Filename UUID | composerId | Rollout filename | Chat session JSON name |
   135→| `type` (user/assistant) | `entry.type` | `bubble.type` (1/2) | Event payloads | TBD |
   136→| `content` | `message.content` | `bubble.text` | Event item text | TBD |
   137→| `toolCalls` | `tool_use` content parts | `toolFormerData` | Tool events | TBD |
   138→| `timestamp` | `entry.timestamp` | `bubble.createdAt` | Event timestamp | TBD |
   139→| `projectPath` | Encoded in directory name | Workspace hash → resolve | CWD from thread.started | Workspace hash → resolve |
   140→
   141→### New Firestore Fields
   142→
   143→To track multi-tool sessions, add to the `sessions` collection:
   144→
   145→```typescript
   146→{
   147→  // ... existing fields ...
   148→  source: 'claude-code' | 'cursor' | 'codex-cli' | 'copilot'  // NEW
   149→  sourceVersion?: string  // e.g., "cursor-0.49", "codex-1.2.0"
   150→}
   151→```
   152→
   153→---
   154→
   155→## Platform Support
   156→
   157→### Current State
   158→
   159→The CLI currently works on **macOS** and should work on **Linux** without changes. **Windows has bugs** that need fixing before multi-tool support:
   160→
   161→| Issue | File | Problem |
   162→|-------|------|---------|
   163→| `process.env.HOME` assumption | `device.ts:7`, `reset.ts:9` | `HOME` not set on Windows; should use `os.homedir()` |
   164→| Hardcoded `/` path separator | `jsonl.ts:206` | `filePath.split('/')` fails on Windows `\` paths |
   165→| Unix path decoding | `jsonl.ts:216-224` | Assumes `-Users-name-path` format; Windows uses `C:\Users\...` |
   166→| `config.ts` | (correct) | Already uses `os.homedir()` — inconsistency, not design |
   167→
   168→### Required Fixes (Pre-Requisite for Multi-Tool)
   169→
   170→1. Replace all `process.env.HOME` with `os.homedir()`
   171→2. Use `path.sep` or `path.basename()` instead of hardcoded `/`
   172→3. Make project path decoding platform-aware
   173→4. Test on Windows (WSL at minimum)
   174→
   175→### Platform Path Matrix (All Tools)
   176→
   177→| Tool | macOS | Linux | Windows |
   178→|------|-------|-------|---------|
   179→| Claude Code | `~/.claude/projects/` | `~/.claude/projects/` | `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\projects\` |
   180→| Cursor | `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/` | `~/.config/Cursor/User/` | `%APPDATA%\Cursor\User\` |
   181→| Codex CLI | `~/.codex/sessions/` | `~/.codex/sessions/` | `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\sessions\` |
   182→| Copilot | `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/` | `~/.config/Code/User/` | `%APPDATA%\Code\User\` |
   183→
   184→---
   185→
   186→## Phased Rollout
   187→
   188→### Phase 1: Cursor Integration (Target first)
   189→
   190→**Why first:** Largest user base among AI coding tools, data is locally accessible, community parsers exist as reference.
   191→
   192→### Phase 2: Codex CLI
   193→
   194→**Why second:** Lowest implementation effort — same JSONL philosophy as Claude Code. Date-organized, local-first, similar pipeline architecture.
   195→
   196→**Effort estimate:** Parser reuse ~70% of existing Claude Code pipeline.
   197→
   198→### Phase 3: GitHub Copilot
   199→
   200→**Why third:** Wait for GitHub to improve their export/history story. In the meantime, consider supporting the SpecStory extension's `.specstory/` directory format as an interim solution.
   201→
   202→### Phase 4: Gemini CLI + Others
   203→
   204→**Why last:** Emerging tools, formats may still be changing. Revisit when they stabilize.
   205→
   206→---
   207→
   208→## Prior Art
   209→
   210→Tools that already tackle multi-tool session parsing:
   211→
   212→| Tool | Focus | Relevant For Us |
   213→|------|-------|-----------------|
   214→| [ccusage](https://github.com/ryoppippi/ccusage) | Token/cost tracking for Claude + Codex | Parser logic for JSONL formats |
   215→| [cass](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search) | Unified search across 11+ tools | Normalization patterns, tool discovery |
   216→| [ccmanager](https://github.com/kbwo/ccmanager) | Session management for 7+ tools | Multi-tool session discovery |
   217→| [cursor-view](https://github.com/saharmor/cursor-view) | Browse/export Cursor chat history | Cursor SQLite parsing |
   218→| [cursor-db-mcp](https://github.com/jbdamask/cursor-db-mcp) | MCP server for Cursor chat history | Cursor key-value schema details |
   219→
   220→---
   221→
   222→## Impact on Vision
   223→
   224→This changes the VISION.md "Non-Goals" section. Currently it says:
   225→
   226→> **Not comprehensive** - Focus on Claude Code, not every AI tool
   227→
   228→This would evolve to:
   229→
   230→> **Tool-agnostic** - Capture insights from any AI coding tool that stores conversations locally
   231→
   232→The core philosophy remains unchanged: your data, your infrastructure, your insights. Multi-tool support just means more data sources feeding the same privacy-first pipeline.
   233→
   234→---
   235→
   236→## Open Questions
   237→
   238→1. **CLI naming**: The npm package is `@code-insights/cli`, not `claude-insights`. Already tool-agnostic in branding.
   239→2. **Dashboard UX**: How to filter/visualize sessions from different tools? New filter? Color-coded by source?
   240→3. **Hook integration**: Claude Code uses hooks for auto-sync. Cursor/Codex have no equivalent — need a watcher or cron-based approach?
   241→4. **Session merging**: If a user works on the same project in both Claude Code and Cursor, should we detect and link related sessions?
   242→5. **Title generation**: Current smart title algorithm is Claude-specific. Need tool-specific title generation strategies.
   243→

1→# CLI v1.2.0 Launch Design
     2→
     3→**Date:** 2026-02-26
     4→**Approach:** Ship & Iterate — Manual QA, fix blockers, npm publish, GitHub release
     5→**Scope:** Full launch with all 4 providers tested, best-effort Windows fixes
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Context
    10→
    11→The CLI (`@code-insights/cli`) is at v1.2.0 but has never been published to npm. Git tags exist for v1.0.0–v1.0.2. Since v1.0.x, significant features were added:
    12→
    13→- **Stats command suite** (5 subcommands: dashboard, cost, projects, today, models)
    14→- **Multi-tool support** (Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI providers)
    15→- **Zero-config mode** (local-first, no Firebase required for stats)
    16→- **Telemetry** (anonymous, opt-out, fire-and-forget)
    17→- **`open` command** (launch web dashboard in browser)
    18→- **Contextual tips & welcome message**
    19→
    20→Current state: 0 test coverage, 0 ESLint config, documented Windows path bugs, no CHANGELOG.

    23→
    24→## Phase 1: QA Test Matrix
    25→
    26→### Provider Discovery & Parse
    27→
    28→| Provider | Test | Verification |
    29→|----------|------|-------------|
    30→| Claude Code | Discovers sessions in `~/.claude/projects/` | `sync --dry-run` shows sessions |
    31→| Claude Code | Parses JSONL (messages, tool calls, timestamps) | Sync + verify in Firestore |
    32→| Claude Code | Smart title generation | Check `generatedTitle` + `titleSource` |
    33→| Cursor | Discovers `.vscdb` in workspace storage | `sync --source cursor --dry-run` |
    34→| Cursor | Parses SQLite tabs | Verify message content |
    35→| Codex CLI | Discovers Codex session files | `sync --source codex-cli --dry-run` |
    36→| Codex CLI | Parses event-stream JSONL | Verify message structure |
    37→| Copilot CLI | Discovers Copilot session files | `sync --source copilot-cli --dry-run` |
    38→| Copilot CLI | Parses event JSONL | Verify message structure |

    41→
    42→| Command | Test Case | Expected |
    43→|---------|-----------|----------|
    44→| `init` | Fresh setup (no config) | Wizard completes, config saved |
    45→| `init --from-json` | Import service account | Config imported, validated |
    46→| `sync` | Normal sync | Sessions uploaded |
    47→| `sync --dry-run` | Dry run | Shows what would sync, no writes |
    48→| `sync --force` | Force re-sync | Re-processes all |

    50→| `status` | Show state | Config, last sync, data source |

    53→| `stats projects` | Project cards | Projects with sparklines |
    54→| `stats today` | Today's sessions | Today's activity |
    55→| `stats models` | Model distribution | Model usage chart |
    56→| `config` | View config | Prints config |
    57→| `config set-source local` | Switch to local | Source updates |
    58→| `config set-source firebase` | Switch to firebase | Source updates |
    59→| `connect` | Generate URL | Base64-encoded URL output |
    60→| `open` | Launch dashboard | Browser opens |
    61→| `install-hook` | Install auto-sync | Hook in Claude Code config |
    62→| `uninstall-hook` | Remove hook | Hook removed |
    63→| `reset --confirm` | Delete data | Firestore cleared |

1→# CLI v1.2.0 Launch — Implementation Plan
     2→
     3→> **For Claude:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.
     4→
     5→**Goal:** QA-test all 4 providers and all commands, fix blockers, polish docs, and publish `@code-insights/cli` v1.2.0 to npm with a GitHub release.

    13→## Phase 1: Fix Blockers

    15→### Task 1: Fix version mismatch in index.ts
    16→
    17→The CLI reports `1.0.0` via `--version` but package.json says `1.2.0`. This is a launch-blocker — users will see the wrong version.
    18→
    19→**Files:**
    20→- Modify: `cli/src/index.ts:21`
    21→
    22→**Step 1: Fix the version string**
    23→
    24→Change line 21 from:
    25→```typescript
    26→  .version('1.0.0');
    27→```
    28→to:
    29→```typescript
    30→  .version('1.2.0');
    31→```
    32→
    33→**Step 2: Verify**
    34→
    35→```bash
    36→cd cli && pnpm build && node dist/index.js --version
    37→```
    38→Expected: `1.2.0`
    39→
    40→**Step 3: Commit**

    97→### Task 3: Fix Windows path issue in jsonl.ts extractProjectName
    98→
    99→`extractProjectName()` at line 355-358 splits on hardcoded `/`. The input comes from `extractProjectPath()` which always returns forward-slash paths (by design — it's decoding Claude's encoded project directory). But `path.basename()` is safer and cleaner.
   100→
   101→**Files:**
   102→- Modify: `cli/src/parser/jsonl.ts:355-358`
   103→
   104→**Step 1: Replace split with path.basename**
   105→
   106→Note: `extractProjectPath()` returns decoded paths like `/Users/name/project`. These always use `/` regardless of platform (they're decoded from Claude's encoding, not from OS paths). However, `path.basename()` handles both separators on Windows, so it's the safer choice.
   107→
   108→Change:
   109→```typescript
   110→function extractProjectName(projectPath: string): string {
   111→  const parts = projectPath.split('/').filter(Boolean);
   112→  return parts[parts.length - 1] || 'unknown';
   113→}
   114→```
   115→to:
   116→```typescript
   117→function extractProjectName(projectPath: string): string {
   118→  // projectPath comes from Claude's encoded format (always forward slashes)
   119→  // path.basename handles both separators cross-platform
   120→  return path.basename(projectPath) || 'unknown';
   121→}
   122→```
   123→
   124→**Step 2: Verify build**
   125→
   126→```bash
   127→cd cli && pnpm build
   128→```
   129→Expected: Clean compile.
   130→
   131→**Step 3: Commit**
   132→
   133→```bash
   134→git add cli/src/parser/jsonl.ts
   135→git commit -m "fix(cli): use path.basename in extractProjectName for Windows compat"
   136→```

     1→# Local-First Architecture Migration Plan
     2→
     3→**Date:** 2026-02-27
     4→**Author:** Technical Architect
     5→**Status:** Draft -- Awaiting Founder Approval
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Executive Summary
    10→
    11→Migrate Code Insights from a two-repo cloud architecture (CLI + hosted web dashboard backed by Firebase/Supabase) to a single-repo local-first architecture. The CLI repo becomes the sole repository. The web dashboard becomes a pre-built SPA served by a local HTTP server via `code-insights dashboard`. Firebase, Supabase, and Vercel are removed entirely. All data lives in SQLite at `~/.code-insights/data.db`. The domain `code-insights.app` becomes a static landing page.
    12→
    13→Think of it like converting a client-server SaaS into a desktop application with a browser UI -- similar to how Grafana or pgAdmin work. The CLI is the engine, SQLite is the database, and a bundled SPA is the windshield.
    14→
    15→### Key Decisions Up Front
    16→
    17→1. **Vite + React SPA** replaces Next.js (no SSR needed for localhost)
    18→2. **Hono** replaces Express for the API server (lightweight, TypeScript-native, works on all runtimes)
    19→3. **better-sqlite3** is already a dependency -- extend it from Cursor-only to the primary data store
    20→4. **All LLM calls move server-side** -- the Hono API proxies them, eliminating browser CORS issues
    21→5. **No auth** -- localhost only, single user
    22→6. **pnpm workspace** for monorepo management (CLI + dashboard packages)
    23→
    24→### Phased Execution Order
    25→
    26→```
    27→Phase 1: Claude Workspace Migration       [S]  ~1 day     No code changes
    28→Phase 2: Data Layer Migration              [L]  ~3-4 days  SQLite schema + write path
    29→Phase 3: Dashboard Embedding               [L]  ~4-5 days  Vite SPA + Hono server + API
    30→Phase 4: Feature Parity Audit + Port       [L]  ~3-4 days  Port all web pages/components
...

1→# Phase 4: Feature Parity Audit — Design Document
     2→
     3→**Date:** 2026-02-27
     4→**Author:** Orchestrator (Brainstorming Session)
     5→**Status:** Approved by Founder
     6→**Parent Plan:** `docs/plans/2026-02-27-local-first-migration.md` (Phase 4)
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Summary
    11→
    12→Port all user-visible features from the web dashboard (`code-insights-web`) to the embedded Vite + React SPA (`dashboard/`). The approach is **layer-by-layer (bottom-up)**: foundation → data → LLM engine → components → pages → polish.
    13→
    14→## Scope
    15→
    16→**Target:** Full feature parity (everything except explicitly dropped items).
    17→
    18→**Included:**
    19→- All dashboard pages (dashboard, sessions, session detail, insights, analytics, settings, export, journal)
    20→- Full chat conversation view with tool panels and markdown rendering
    21→- LLM analysis engine ported to server (all 4 providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama)
    22→- CLI command for LLM provider configuration (`code-insights config llm`)
    23→- Session rename, analyze, bulk analyze, prompt quality analysis
    24→- Multi-source filter support (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI)
    25→- Dark mode with FOUC prevention
    26→- Guided empty states (replaces demo mode)
    27→
    28→**Excluded:**
    29→- Authentication (login, auth callback, Supabase middleware)
    30→- Firebase/Firestore config flow (ConfigDialog, FirebaseSetupGuide, FirebaseConfigForm)
    31→- Marketing/landing page components (11 files)
    32→- Demo mode data/banner
    33→- Framer Motion animations (CSS transitions only)
    34→- Gemini EnhanceButton (evaluated as redundant with general LLM analysis)
    35→- Export reminders/scheduler
    36→- SSE real-time updates (v3.1 — polling at 2s for v3.0)
    37→
    38→## Decisions
    39→
    40→| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    41→|----------|--------|-----------|
    42→| Execution approach | Layer-by-layer (bottom-up) | Each layer testable before next builds on it |
    43→| LLM integration | Full server-side port | End-to-end analysis works. Config via CLI and UI. |

1→# PostHog Telemetry Migration — Design
     2→
     3→**Date:** 2026-03-02
     4→**Status:** Approved (Founder + TA)
     5→**Replaces:** Supabase Edge Function telemetry (cli-telemetry-v2)
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Problem
    10→
    11→The current telemetry system sends raw events to a Supabase Edge Function with no analytics UI. Monthly-rotating machine IDs prevent accurate unique user counts. Key signals are missing: performance timing, exact session counts, per-provider sync breakdown, LLM provider usage, and dashboard engagement.
    12→
    13→## Solution
    14→
    15→Migrate to PostHog with two SDKs (posthog-node for CLI/server, posthog-js for dashboard SPA), a stable machine identity, and an expanded event schema that captures what we need to understand adoption, feature usage, and performance.
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## Architecture
    20→
    21→```
    22→CLI commands ──► posthog-node (server-side) ──► PostHog Cloud
    23→                     ▲
    24→Server routes ───────┘
    25→                                                     ▲
    26→Dashboard SPA ──► posthog-js (client-side) ──────────┘
    27→```
    28→
    29→- **posthog-node** (~20KB, no native deps) in `cli/package.json` — used by CLI commands and server-side Hono routes via existing `@code-insights/cli/utils/telemetry` import.
    30→- **posthog-js** (~45KB) in `dashboard/package.json` — used by dashboard SPA for page views and load timing.
    31→- **Shared identity:** Server exposes `GET /api/telemetry/identity` so the SPA uses the same `distinct_id` as CLI/server.
    32→- **Supabase fully removed:** Endpoint URL, HMAC signing key, `signPayload()`, and edge function are deleted.
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## Identity & Privacy
    37→
    38→### Stable Machine ID
    39→
    40→```
    41→SHA-256(hostname:username:code-insights)[0:16]
    42→```
    43→
    44→- No monthly salt — same machine always produces the same ID
    45→- No PII transmitted — hostname and username are hashed, never sent
    46→- Deterministic — survives reinstalls, no stored file needed
    47→- PostHog configured with `ip: false` on both SDKs to discard IP addresses
    48→
    49→### Opt-Out Mechanisms (preserved)
    50→
    51→1. `CODE_INSIGHTS_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` env var
    52→2. `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` env var (community standard)
    53→3. `code-insights telemetry disable` (persists to config)
    54→
    55→### Dashboard SPA Opt-Out
    56→
    57→`GET /api/telemetry/identity` returns `{ enabled: false }` when telemetry is disabled. The SPA does not initialize posthog-js at all.
    58→
    59→### Disclosure Notice
    60→
    61→Re-shown to all existing users. The `.telemetry-notice-shown` touch file is version-stamped (CLI version written as content). If version < this release, notice re-shown.
    62→
    63→Updated text:
    64→```
    65→Code Insights collects anonymous usage data to improve the CLI and dashboard.
    66→Includes: commands, page views, OS, CLI version, AI tool types, session counts,
    67→LLM provider, performance timing.
    68→Never includes: file paths, project names, session content, API keys, or personal data.
    69→```
    70→
    71→---
    72→
    73→## Event Schema
    74→
    75→### Common Properties (registered once via posthog.identify)
    76→
    77→| Property | Example | Source |
    78→|----------|---------|--------|
    79→| cli_version | 3.3.0 | package.json |
    80→| node_version | 22.5.1 | process.version |

1→# Technical Architect Memory (Archived from code-insights-web)
     2→
     3→> Archived on 2026-02-27 as part of the local-first migration (Phase 1).
     4→> Original location: `code-insights-web/.claude/agent-memory/technical-architect/MEMORY.md`
     5→
     6→## Project Architecture (Verified 2026-02-19)
     7→
     8→### Cross-Repo Type Contract
     9→- CLI types: `../code-insights/cli/src/types.ts` (source of truth)
    10→- Web types: `src/lib/types.ts` (mirror, manually synced)
    11→- Key difference: CLI `ToolCall.input` is `Record<string, unknown>`, web is `string` (serialized on write)
    12→- All new Firestore fields MUST be optional for backward compatibility
    13→
    14→### Firestore Collections
    15→- `projects`, `sessions`, `messages` written by CLI (Admin SDK)
    16→- `insights` written by web (Client SDK)
    17→- `stats/usage` singleton doc written by CLI (atomic increments)
    18→- Usage stats fields on sessions/projects are optional (added after launch)
    19→
    20→### Auth Architecture
    21→- Supabase Auth for authentication only (no user data in Supabase)
    22→- Firebase BYOF for all user data (no Firebase Auth used)
    23→- Middleware uses `getUser()` not `getSession()` for server-side JWT validation
    24→
    25→### LLM Analysis
    26→- Runs in browser, user's API keys in localStorage
    27→- 4 providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama
    28→- Token cap: 80,000 input. Chunks messages if exceeded.
    29→- Analysis version: 2.0.0
    30→- Anthropic needs `anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access: true` header
    31→
    32→### CLI Sync Pipeline
    33→- Discovers JSONL in `~/.claude/projects/` (including `subagents/` subdirs)
    34→- Sync state tracks file mtime (not line position)
    35→- Crash-safe: state saved after each file
    36→- `--force` triggers full recalculation of usage stats
    37→
    38→### Key Gaps (Remaining)
    39→- No incremental line tracking (re-parses full files)
    40→- No concurrent sync protection (lock file)
    41→- Analytics hooks limited to 500 sessions / 1000 insights
    42→- See `docs/architecture/11-gaps-and-improvements.md` for full list
    43→
    44→### Completed Gaps (as of 2026-02-20)
    45→- Thinking content + tool results: **DONE** (separate fields on Message, not appended to content)
    46→- Per-message usage (MessageUsage): **DONE** on assistant messages
    47→- sourceTool field: **DONE** (`'claude-code'` set by CLI)
    48→- Session delete: **DONE** (`src/lib/firestore/sessions.ts`, cascading)
    49→- Per-session export: **DONE** (Download dropdown, 3 formats)
    50→- Recurring insight detection: **DONE** (findRecurringInsights + linkedInsightIds)

1→# Design: Fix Dashboard Analysis Flow
     2→
     3→> **Date:** 2026-03-02
     4→> **Status:** Approved (TA + UX reviewed)
     5→> **Scope:** Server SSE endpoints + Dashboard AnalysisContext rewrite + bug fixes
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Problem
    10→
    11→The CLI dashboard's analysis flow has two critical bugs and several missing features compared to the original web repo implementation (deleted in PR #104 of code-insights-web):
    12→
    13→1. **AnalysisProvider not in React tree** — `useAnalysis()` returns the default no-op context, so clicking "Analyze" calls `async () => {}`. Silent no-op.
    14→2. **Global polling every 5 seconds** — `refetchInterval: 5000` in `main.tsx` causes every React Query hook to re-fetch constantly. On session detail pages: 3 endpoints x every 5s = massive waste for immutable data.
    15→3. **No per-chunk progress** — Current state only has `{ message: string }`, no phase/chunk tracking.
    16→4. **Cancel is cosmetic** — Resets React state but server continues processing the LLM call.
    17→5. **No insight count or token usage** — Success message is generic "Analysis complete!".
    18→6. **AI title suggestion broken** — Banner UI exists but never triggers because `result.insights` is never populated.
    19→
    20→## Design
    21→
    22→### Fix 1: Add AnalysisProvider to React Tree
    23→
    24→**File:** `dashboard/src/main.tsx`
    25→
    26→Wrap `<App />` with `<AnalysisProvider>` inside `QueryClientProvider` (uses `useQueryClient`):
    27→
    28→```tsx
    29→<QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
    30→  <ThemeProvider defaultTheme="system">
    31→    <AnalysisProvider>
    32→      <App />
    33→    </AnalysisProvider>
    34→  </ThemeProvider>
    35→</QueryClientProvider>
    36→```
    37→
    38→Placed in `main.tsx` (not `Layout.tsx`) so analysis state survives route transitions.
    39→
    40→### Fix 2: Remove Global Polling
    41→
    42→**File:** `dashboard/src/main.tsx`
    43→
    44→```tsx
    45→const queryClient = new QueryClient({
    46→  defaultOptions: {
    47→    queries: {
    48→      staleTime: 30_000,
    49→      retry: 1,
    50→    },
    51→  },
    52→});
    53→```
    54→
    55→- Remove `refetchInterval: 5000` entirely
    56→- Set `staleTime: 30_000` (local SQLite data, only changes on sync or analysis)
    57→- `refetchOnWindowFocus: true` is React Query's default — handles the "user tabs back" case
    58→- Data freshness after mutations handled by `queryClient.invalidateQueries()` (already in place)
    59→- No polling anywhere, not even dashboard stats
    60→

1→# Test Suite Design — Code Insights
     2→
     3→> Full test suite for CLI and server packages using Vitest.
     4→
     5→**Date:** 2026-02-28
     6→**Status:** Approved
     7→**Scope:** CLI + Server (dashboard deferred)
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Decisions
    12→
    13→| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    14→|----------|--------|-----------|
    15→| Framework | Vitest | Native ESM, Vite-native for dashboard (future), fast, monorepo workspace support |
    16→| Structure | Monorepo-unified | Root `vitest.workspace.ts` + per-package configs. Single `pnpm test` |
    17→| File convention | Co-located `*.test.ts` | Tests next to source for discoverability |
    18→| DB testing | In-memory SQLite | Real SQL engine, no mocks, fast |
    19→| Server testing | Hono `app.request()` | No HTTP server needed, tests route handlers directly |
    20→| FS mocking | `vi.mock('fs')` + fixtures | Controlled, deterministic, fast |
    21→| Dashboard | Deferred | CLI and server are where data integrity bugs live |
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## Infrastructure
    26→
    27→### Dependencies
    28→
    29→| Package | New Dev Dependencies |
    30→|---------|---------------------|
    31→| Root | `vitest` |
    32→| CLI | (uses root vitest) |
    33→| Server | (uses root vitest) |
    34→
    35→### Config Files
    36→
    37→```
    38→code-insights/
    39→├── vitest.workspace.ts          # Lists ['cli', 'server']
    40→├── cli/vitest.config.ts         # environment: 'node'
    41→├── server/vitest.config.ts      # environment: 'node'
    42→```
    43→
    44→### Scripts
    45→
    46→```json
    47→// root package.json
    48→"test": "vitest run",
    49→"test:watch": "vitest",
    50→"test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage"

  - Configuration system at `~/.code-insights/config.json`

- [x] **1.2 Claude Code Parser**
  - Parse Claude Code session files from `~/.claude/projects/`
  - Extract: sessions, messages, tool calls, timestamps
  - Smart session title generation (5-tier fallback)
  - Session character classification (7 types)
  - CLI command: `code-insights sync`

- [x] **1.3 SQLite Schema & Sync**
  - Local database at `~/.code-insights/data.db` (WAL mode)
  - Tables: projects, sessions, messages, insights
  - Incremental sync (tracks file modification times in `sync-state.json`)
  - Stable project IDs derived from git remote URLs
  - CLI command: `code-insights init`

- [x] **1.4 Basic Dashboard**
  - Session list view with filters (project, date)
  - Session detail view with message display
  - Insights display by type
  - Analytics page with Recharts charts

### Deliverables
- ✅ CLI tool that syncs sessions to local SQLite
- ✅ Local web dashboard served by `code-insights dashboard`

---

## Phase 2: Integration ✅

**Goal:** Seamless integration with Claude Code workflow and terminal analytics

### Milestones

- [x] **2.1 Claude Code Hook**
  - Post-session hook that triggers sync automatically
  - Quiet mode for background processing (`sync -q`)
  - `code-insights install-hook` / `code-insights uninstall-hook`

- [x] **2.2 CLI Stats Command Suite**
  - Terminal analytics: `stats`, `stats cost`, `stats projects`, `stats today`, `stats models`
  - Powered by local SQLite — zero external dependencies
  - Unicode sparklines, bar charts, semantic colors, responsive layout
  - Shared flags: `--period`, `--project`, `--source`, `--no-sync`

- [ ] **2.3 Enhanced Filtering**
  - Full-text search across sessions
  - Filter by: project, git branch, date range, insight type, source tool
  - Saved filters / bookmarks

### Deliverables
- ✅ Auto-sync via Claude Code hook
- ✅ CLI stats command suite with local analytics
- Enhanced filtering (pending)

---

## Phase 3: Intelligence ✅

**Goal:** LLM-powered deeper insights

### Milestones

- [x] **3.1 Multi-Provider LLM Integration**
  - Pluggable provider system (factory pattern)
  - OpenAI (gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4-turbo)
  - Anthropic (claude-sonnet, claude-haiku, claude-opus)
  - Google Gemini (gemini-2.0-flash, gemini-1.5-pro/flash)
  - Ollama for local models (llama3.2, mistral, codellama)
  - LLM calls proxied server-side via Hono API (no browser CORS)
  - Token input capped at 80k

- [x] **3.2 Session Analysis**
  - "Analyze" button on session detail page
  - Bulk analyze for unanalyzed sessions
  - Generates 4 insight types: summary, decision, learning, technique
  - Analysis versioning for re-analysis

- [ ] **3.3 Cross-Session Patterns**
  - Cross-session pattern detection
  - Project-level and overall-level insights
  - Recurring pattern identification

- [ ] **3.4 Learning Journal**
  - Auto-generate "lessons learned" from sessions
  - Track recurring patterns and mistakes
  - Suggest areas for improvement

### Deliverables
- ✅ Multi-provider LLM insight generation
- ✅ On-demand and bulk session analysis
- Cross-session patterns (pending)
- Learning journal (pending)

---

## Phase 4: Feature Parity ✅

**Goal:** Port all dashboard features to the embedded local SPA and add multi-source support

### Milestones

- [x] **4.1 Vite + React SPA Foundation**
  - Vite SPA replacing Next.js (no SSR needed for localhost)
  - Hono API server embedding the SPA
  - React Query for server state, Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui

- [x] **4.2 Multi-Source Provider Support**
  - `SessionProvider` interface for source tool abstraction
  - Providers: `claude-code`, `cursor`, `codex-cli`, `copilot-cli`
  - `--source` filter flag in all stats and sync commands
  - Source tool display in dashboard (colors, avatars, filters)

- [x] **4.3 Dashboard Feature Parity**
  - Sessions list with source, project, date, character filters
  - Session detail with full message display and analyze button
  - Analytics page with cost, models, projects breakdown
  - Stats overview and project cards

- [ ] **4.4 Markdown Export**
  - Export by: session, day, week, project
  - Formats: Plain Markdown, Obsidian (with wikilinks), Notion
  - Export page in dashboard

### Deliverables
- ✅ Embedded local dashboard via `code-insights dashboard`
- ✅ Multi-source support (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI)
- ✅ Full feature parity with original hosted dashboard
- Markdown export (in progress)

---

## Phase 5: Telemetry (In Progress)

**Goal:** Anonymous, opt-in usage signals to improve the tool without compromising privacy

### Milestones

- [ ] **5.1 Anonymous Aggregate Signals**
  - Opt-in only, disabled by default
  - Local export only — no phone-home during normal usage
  - Depth metrics: sessions analyzed, insights generated

- [ ] **5.2 Slash Commands**
  - `/insights` — Quick summary of recent sessions
  - `/insights today` — What you worked on today
  - `/insights decisions` — Recent architectural decisions

### Deliverables
- Anonymous telemetry (opt-in, pending)
- Slash commands (pending)

---

## Phase 6: Polish & Distribution

**Goal:** Production-ready distribution and community foundation

### Milestones

- [ ] **6.1 npm Distribution**
  - Publish as `@code-insights/cli`
  - Binary: `code-insights`
  - Pre-built dashboard SPA bundled in the package

- [ ] **6.2 Plugin Architecture**
  - Custom insight extractors
  - Dashboard widget API
  - Theme support

- [ ] **6.3 Community**
  - Static landing page at `code-insights.app`
  - Comprehensive setup guide and API documentation
  - Community-contributed export templates

### Deliverables
- Published npm package
- Plugin architecture foundation
- Community documentation

---

## Version Milestones

| Version | Phase | Key Features | Status |
|---------|-------|--------------|--------|
| 0.1.0 | 1 | CLI sync, SQLite schema, basic dashboard | ✅ Done |
| 0.2.0 | 1 | Smart titles, session classification | ✅ Done |
| 0.3.0 | 2 | Claude Code hook, CLI stats commands | ✅ Done |
| 0.4.0 | 3 | Multi-LLM analysis, bulk analyze | ✅ Done |
| 0.5.0 | 4 | Vite SPA + Hono server, embedded dashboard | ✅ Done |
| 0.6.0 | 4 | Multi-source support (Cursor, Codex, Copilot CLI) | ✅ Done |
| 0.7.0 | 4-5 | Markdown export, slash commands | Planned |
| 1.0.0 | 6 | npm publish, plugin architecture | Planned |

---

## Contributing

This is an open source project. Contributions welcome!

- **Issues**: Bug reports, feature requests
- **PRs**: Code contributions (please discuss first for large changes)
- **Docs**: Improvements to documentation
- **Providers**: New source tool providers

See CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines.

# Code Insights Vision

## Philosophy

**Your data, your machine, your insights.**

Code Insights is a free, open-source tool that helps developers who use multiple AI coding tools analyze their sessions, collect insights, track decisions and learnings, and build knowledge over time. It's built on a simple principle: your session data never leaves your machine.

## Core Beliefs

### 1. Privacy by Architecture

There is no central Code Insights server. No accounts, no sign-ups, no cloud. All session data lives in a local SQLite database at `~/.code-insights/data.db`. The dashboard runs locally at `http://localhost:7890` — it never phones home.

### 2. Developers Can Handle It

Developers using AI coding tools are technical. They can:
- Run `code-insights init` and answer three questions
- Install a post-session hook with one command
- Open a local dashboard that just works

We don't need to hide complexity behind a managed service. Clear documentation beats magic.

### 3. Single-Repo, Local-First

Everything ships in one repository:
- **CLI** (open source, MIT) — the parser, sync engine, and stats commands
- **Dashboard** (embedded SPA) — served locally by a Hono server via `code-insights dashboard`
- **Server** (local API) — Hono API on `localhost:7890`, proxies LLM calls server-side

No hosted infrastructure. No Vercel. No Firebase. No Supabase. One install, zero cloud dependencies.

### 4. Tool, Not Platform

Code Insights is a utility, not a product. It should:
- Do one thing well (extract insights from AI coding sessions)
- Support multiple source tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI)
- Be easy to install and configure
- Stay out of the way once set up

## Long-Term Direction

### Phase 1: Foundation ✅
- CLI tool that parses JSONL → SQLite
- Web dashboard with session views, character classification, smart titles
- Claude Code hook for automatic session sync

### Phase 2: Integration ✅
- Auto-sync via Claude Code post-session hook
- CLI stats command suite (`stats`, `stats cost`, `stats projects`, `stats today`, `stats models`)
- Terminal analytics powered by local SQLite

### Phase 3: Intelligence ✅
- Multi-provider LLM analysis (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama)
- On-demand and bulk session analysis
- Cross-session insight types (summary, decision, learning, technique)

### Phase 4: Feature Parity ✅
- Vite + React SPA replacing the hosted web dashboard
- Hono server embedding the SPA — served via `code-insights dashboard`
- Multi-source support: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot CLI
- Full feature parity between CLI stats and dashboard views

### Phase 5: Telemetry (In Progress)
- Anonymous aggregate usage signals (opt-in, local-only export)
- Depth metrics: sessions analyzed, insights generated, knowledge retained

### Phase 6: Polish & Distribution
- npm publish as `@code-insights/cli`
- Static landing page at `code-insights.app`
- Contribution guidelines and plugin architecture foundation

## Non-Goals

- **Not a business** — No monetization, no paywall, no premium tier
- **Not a central platform** — No central database for user session data
- **Not a dependency** — Users can stop using it anytime, data remains theirs
- **Not a team tool** — This is a personal learning tool; no org/team features

## Success Looks Like

A developer installs Code Insights, runs `code-insights init`, installs the hook, and from then on has a local dashboard showing:
- What they built with AI coding tools this week
- Key decisions and why they made them
- Patterns in how they use AI assistance across tools

They own all the data. They can export it. They can delete it. They can modify the CLI tool. Complete autonomy.

file.content:
# Code Insights

## What It Is

Code Insights transforms your AI coding session history into structured, searchable insights. It extracts patterns from your conversations—what you built, decisions you made, lessons learned—and presents them in a local visual dashboard.

## The Problem

AI coding tools store every conversation as files on your machine. Claude Code uses JSONL in `~/.claude/projects/`. Cursor stores state in SQLite. Codex CLI and Copilot CLI write their own session formats. This is valuable data:
- What features did you work on last week?
- Why did you choose that architecture?
- What mistakes did you make (and fix)?
- How much time went into different parts of the codebase?

But it's trapped in raw files. You can't search it, visualize it, or learn from it.

## The Solution

Code Insights provides:

1. **Automated extraction** — Parses session files from multiple AI coding tools and structures the data
2. **Smart session titles** — Auto-generates meaningful titles from session content
3. **Session classification** — Categorizes sessions (deep focus, bug hunt, feature build, etc.)
4. **LLM-powered analysis** — Multi-provider insight generation (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama) with your own API key
5. **Visual dashboard** — Local web interface with charts, timelines, and filters at `http://localhost:7890`
6. **CLI analytics** — Terminal stats via `code-insights stats` and subcommands

## Who It's For

- **Developers using multiple AI coding tools** who want to understand their AI-assisted work patterns across Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, and Copilot CLI
- **Learners** who want to review and reinforce what they've built with AI assistants
- **Privacy-conscious developers** who want insights without giving up their data to a cloud service

## Privacy Model

**Fully local. No cloud. No accounts.**

Code Insights stores all session data in a SQLite database at `~/.code-insights/data.db` on your own machine. There is no central server, no sign-up, and no data sent anywhere. The dashboard runs locally at `http://localhost:7890` — served by a Hono API process on your own machine.

LLM analysis uses your own API key, stored in `~/.code-insights/config.json` (mode 0o600). API calls go directly from the local server to your chosen LLM provider — not through any Code Insights infrastructure.

## Core Features

### Multi-Source Support

| Source Tool | What's Captured |
|-------------|-----------------|
| **Claude Code** | JSONL sessions from `~/.claude/projects/` |
| **Cursor** | Sessions from Cursor's local SQLite state |
| **Codex CLI** | Rollout files from `~/.codex/sessions/` |
| **Copilot CLI** | Event files from `~/.copilot/session-state/` |

### Insight Categories

| Category | What It Captures |
|----------|------------------|
| **Summary** | High-level narrative of what was accomplished |
| **Decision** | Architecture choices, trade-offs, reasoning, alternatives |
| **Learning** | Technical discoveries, mistakes, transferable knowledge |
| **Technique** | Problem-solving approaches and debugging strategies |

### Dashboard Views

- **Daily/Weekly digest** — Summary of recent sessions
- **Project timeline** — Visual history of work per project
- **Decision log** — Searchable archive of "why" decisions
- **Analytics** — Charts showing effort distribution, patterns
- **Session detail** — Full session with analyze button for LLM insights

### CLI Stats Commands

```bash
code-insights stats              # Overview (last 7 days)
code-insights stats cost         # Cost breakdown by project and model
code-insights stats projects     # Per-project detail cards
code-insights stats today        # Today's sessions with details
code-insights stats models       # Model usage distribution
```

## Tech Stack

- **CLI**: Node.js (ES2022, ES Modules), Commander.js
- **Database**: SQLite (`better-sqlite3`) at `~/.code-insights/data.db` — WAL mode, local
- **Server**: Hono — lightweight API server, serves dashboard SPA at `localhost:7890`
- **Dashboard**: Vite + React 19 SPA, Tailwind CSS 4 + shadcn/ui
- **AI**: Multi-provider — OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama (your own API keys, proxied server-side)
- **Package manager**: pnpm (workspace monorepo: `cli/`, `dashboard/`, `server/`)

## Success Metrics

- Time to first insight: < 5 minutes from install
- User can answer "what did I work on this week?" in one click
- Decisions are searchable and linkable
- Zero cloud dependencies after install

content.content:
1→# Multi-Tool Vision: Beyond Claude Code
     2→
     3→> Expanding Code Insights to capture sessions from all AI coding tools.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Motivation
     8→
     9→Code Insights was built to capture insights from Claude Code sessions. But developers increasingly use multiple AI coding tools — sometimes within the same project. The conversation data from these tools is trapped in proprietary formats, scattered across different locations, and inaccessible for reflection.
    10→
    11→**The opportunity:** Become the universal session insights layer for AI-assisted development, regardless of which tool generated the conversation.
    12→
    13→## Target Tools
    14→
    15→| Tool | Type | Storage Format | Local? | Feasibility |
    16→|------|------|---------------|--------|-------------|
    17→| Claude Code | CLI | JSONL | Yes | **Already supported** |
    18→| Cursor | IDE (VS Code fork) | SQLite + JSON blobs | Yes | **High** (Phase 1) |
    19→| OpenAI Codex CLI | CLI | JSONL | Yes | **High** (Phase 2) |
    20→| GitHub Copilot | VS Code extension | JSON in workspaceStorage | Yes | **Medium-Low** (Phase 3) |
    21→| Gemini CLI | CLI | TBD (investigate) | TBD | **Unknown** (Phase 4) |
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→## How Each Tool Stores Conversations
    26→
    27→### Claude Code (Current)
    28→
    29→- **Location**: `~/.claude/projects/<encoded-project-path>/<session-id>.jsonl`
    30→- **Format**: JSONL — one JSON object per line, append-only
    31→- **Entry types**: `user`, `assistant`, `system`, `summary`
    32→- **Key fields**: `uuid`, `type`, `message.content`, `timestamp`, `cwd`, `gitBranch`, `version`
    33→- **Session discovery**: Walk `~/.claude/projects/` directories, each `.jsonl` file is one session
    34→- **Platform paths**:
    35→  - macOS/Linux: `~/.claude/projects/`
    36→  - Windows: `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\projects\`
    37→
    38→### Cursor
    39→
    40→- **Location**: SQLite databases (`state.vscdb`) in two locations:
    41→  - Global: `<cursor-data-dir>/globalStorage/state.vscdb`
    42→  - Per-workspace: `<cursor-data-dir>/workspaceStorage/<hash>/state.vscdb`
    43→- **Platform-specific data directory**:
    44→  - macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/`
    45→  - Linux: `~/.config/Cursor/User/`
    46→  - Windows: `%APPDATA%\Cursor\User\`
    47→- **Format**: SQLite key-value table (`ItemTable`) with JSON blobs as values
    48→- **Key DB keys**:
    49→  - `composer.composerData` — primary chat list (v3 format)
    50→  - `composerData:<composerId>` — individual conversation data
    51→  - `workbench.panel.aichat.view.aichat.chatdata` — legacy chat storage
    52→- **Message schema** (composerData v3):
    53→  ```json
    54→  {
    55→    "bubbleId": "uuid",
    56→    "type": 2,
    57→    "createdAt": "ISO timestamp",
    58→    "text": "message content",
    59→    "codeBlocks": [...],
    60→    "toolFormerData": {...},
    61→    "thinking": {...}
    62→  }
    63→  ```
    64→  - `type: 1` = user message, `type: 2` = assistant response
    65→- **Challenges**:

    85→

    89→  - Windows: `%APPDATA%\Code\User\workspaceStorage/<hash>/chatSessions/*.json`

    97→- **Workarounds**: Third-party extensions like SpecStory auto-save to `.specstory/` directories
    98→
    99→---
   100→
   101→## Architecture: Provider Abstraction
   102→
   103→The CLI needs a **provider abstraction** — a common interface that each tool's parser implements:
   104→
   105→```
   106→┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   107→│                    CLI Pipeline                           │
   108→│                                                          │
   109→│  ┌─────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌────────────┐  │
   110→│  │  Discovery   │───▶│   Parser     │───▶│  Sync to   │  │
   111→│  │  (find files)│    │  (normalize) │    │  Firestore │  │
   112→│  └─────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └────────────┘  │
   113→│         │                  │                              │
   114→│         ▼                  ▼                              │
   115→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────┐                     │
   116→│  │      Provider Interface         │                     │
   117→│  │                                 │                     │
   118→│  │  discover() → FilePath[]        │                     │
   119→│  │  parse(file) → ParsedSession    │                     │
   120→│  │  getProviderName() → string     │                     │
   121→│  └─────────────────────────────────┘                     │
   122→│         ▲         ▲         ▲                            │
   123→│  ┌──────┴──┐ ┌────┴────┐ ┌─┴───────┐                    │
   124→│  │ Claude  │ │ Cursor  │ │ Codex   │  ...                │
   125→│  │Provider │ │Provider │ │Provider │                     │
   126→│  └─────────┘ └─────────┘ └─────────┘                    │
   127→└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   128→```
   129→
   130→Each provider must normalize its native format into the existing `ParsedSession` and `ParsedMessage` types. This means mapping tool-specific concepts to our schema:
   131→
   132→| Our Field | Claude Code | Cursor | Codex CLI | Copilot |
   133→|-----------|-------------|--------|-----------|---------|
   134→| `sessionId` | Filename UUID | composerId | Rollout filename | Chat session JSON name |
   135→| `type` (user/assistant) | `entry.type` | `bubble.type` (1/2) | Event payloads | TBD |
   136→| `content` | `message.content` | `bubble.text` | Event item text | TBD |
   137→| `toolCalls` | `tool_use` content parts | `toolFormerData` | Tool events | TBD |
   138→| `timestamp` | `entry.timestamp` | `bubble.createdAt` | Event timestamp | TBD |
   139→| `projectPath` | Encoded in directory name | Workspace hash → resolve | CWD from thread.started | Workspace hash → resolve |
   140→
   141→### New Firestore Fields
   142→
   143→To track multi-tool sessions, add to the `sessions` collection:
   144→
   145→```typescript
   146→{
   147→  // ... existing fields ...
   148→  source: 'claude-code' | 'cursor' | 'codex-cli' | 'copilot'  // NEW
   149→  sourceVersion?: string  // e.g., "cursor-0.49", "codex-1.2.0"
   150→}
   151→```
   152→
   153→---
   154→
   155→## Platform Support
   156→
   157→### Current State
   158→
   159→The CLI currently works on **macOS** and should work on **Linux** without changes. **Windows has bugs** that need fixing before multi-tool support:
   160→
   161→| Issue | File | Problem |
   162→|-------|------|---------|
   163→| `process.env.HOME` assumption | `device.ts:7`, `reset.ts:9` | `HOME` not set on Windows; should use `os.homedir()` |
   164→| Hardcoded `/` path separator | `jsonl.ts:206` | `filePath.split('/')` fails on Windows `\` paths |
   165→| Unix path decoding | `jsonl.ts:216-224` | Assumes `-Users-name-path` format; Windows uses `C:\Users\...` |
   166→| `config.ts` | (correct) | Already uses `os.homedir()` — inconsistency, not design |
   167→

   169→
   170→1. Replace all `process.env.HOME` with `os.homedir()`
   171→2. Use `path.sep` or `path.basename()` instead of hardcoded `/`
   172→3. Make project path decoding platform-aware
   173→4. Test on Windows (WSL at minimum)
   174→
   175→### Platform Path Matrix (All Tools)
   176→
   177→| Tool | macOS | Linux | Windows |
   178→|------|-------|-------|---------|
   179→| Claude Code | `~/.claude/projects/` | `~/.claude/projects/` | `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\projects\` |
   180→| Cursor | `~/Library/Application Support/Cursor/User/` | `~/.config/Cursor/User/` | `%APPDATA%\Cursor\User\` |
   181→| Codex CLI | `~/.codex/sessions/` | `~/.codex/sessions/` | `%USERPROFILE%\.codex\sessions\` |
   182→| Copilot | `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/` | `~/.config/Code/User/` | `%APPDATA%\Code\User\` |
   183→
   184→---

   186→## Phased Rollout
   187→
   188→### Phase 1: Cursor Integration (Target first)
   189→
   190→**Why first:** Largest user base among AI coding tools, data is locally accessible, community parsers exist as reference.
   191→
   192→### Phase 2: Codex CLI
   193→
   194→**Why second:** Lowest implementation effort — same JSONL philosophy as Claude Code. Date-organized, local-first, similar pipeline architecture.
   195→
   196→**Effort estimate:** Parser reuse ~70% of existing Claude Code pipeline.
   197→
   198→### Phase 3: GitHub Copilot
   199→
   200→**Why third:** Wait for GitHub to improve their export/history story. In the meantime, consider supporting the SpecStory extension's `.specstory/` directory format as an interim solution.
   201→
   202→### Phase 4: Gemini CLI + Others
   203→

   205→
   206→---
   207→
   208→## Prior Art
   209→
   210→Tools that already tackle multi-tool session parsing:
   211→
   212→| Tool | Focus | Relevant For Us |
   213→|------|-------|-----------------|
   214→| [ccusage](https://github.com/ryoppippi/ccusage) | Token/cost tracking for Claude + Codex | Parser logic for JSONL formats |
   215→| [cass](https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search) | Unified search across 11+ tools | Normalization patterns, tool discovery |
   216→| [ccmanager](https://github.com/kbwo/ccmanager) | Session management for 7+ tools | Multi-tool session discovery |
   217→| [cursor-view](https://github.com/saharmor/cursor-view) | Browse/export Cursor chat history | Cursor SQLite parsing |
   218→| [cursor-db-mcp](https://github.com/jbdamask/cursor-db-mcp) | MCP server for Cursor chat history | Cursor key-value schema details |
   219→
   220→---
   221→
   222→## Impact on Vision

   225→

   227→

   229→
   230→> **Tool-agnostic** - Capture insights from any AI coding tool that stores conversations locally

   239→2. **Dashboard UX**: How to filter/visualize sessions from different tools? New filter? Color-coded by source?
   240→3. **Hook integration**: Claude Code uses hooks for auto-sync. Cursor/Codex have no equivalent — need a watcher or cron-based approach?
